assertions.reason: A live mocked relay-bot run dispatched three prompts at the same instant and the wrapper input log recorded all three turn/start payloads in the original order (rapid prompt one, two, three), with matching echo messages reaching the attached chat.

The pending approval scenario moved the relay session to waitingApproval with a stored serverRequest lastMessage, and on attach the bot sent a formatted approval prompt containing the shell action, command, path, request ID, and y/n instructions.

The same end-to-end run sent approve and deny replies through the bot, and the wrapper input log captured both codex-bound responses exactly as {"id":"req-pending","result":{"decision":"accept"}} and {"id":"req-deny","result":{"decision":"decline"}}.

After a remote bot prompt, the wrapper input log showed the injected turn/start prompt, the relay session and bot attachment both remained attached to the same user, and the messenger transcript contained no auto-detach notice before or after the rapid prompt burst.

With no attached user, a serverRequest/approval was retained in session history and left the relay session in waitingApproval; when the user later attached, the bot immediately surfaced that pending approval prompt to the chat.

commands.command: npm --prefix "/data/dl/fschannel/shared" run build && npm --prefix "/data/dl/fschannel/server" run build && npm --prefix "/data/dl/fschannel/bot" run build && cargo build --manifest-path "/data/dl/fschannel/wrapper/Cargo.toml"
commands.observation: Built the shared/server/bot TypeScript artifacts and confirmed the wrapper debug binary was available before running cross-area validations.

commands.command: npm --prefix "/data/dl/fschannel/bot" exec -- vitest run "src/integration-core-flows.test.ts" -t "runs approval flows end-to-end through the bot service with a mocked messenger"
commands.observation: Vitest passed the targeted approval flow integration test (1 passed, 4 skipped), covering pending approval presentation, approve/deny relay, and attachment retention on a remote prompt.

commands.command: node --input-type=module <<'NODE' [mocked relay-bot approval + rapid prompt evidence script] NODE
commands.observation: A live mocked integration run captured the approval prompt text, pre-attach waitingApproval state, approve/deny wrapper input entries, a remote prompt that kept attachment active, and three rapid prompts observed in wrapper input order one/two/three.

summary: Validated 5 cross-area approval/queuing assertions for the relay-bot integration surface; all assigned assertions passed.
